creator: bofh@freebsd.org
text:
Despite my personal reply you have submitted this as a bug so I will continue the thread here.

The issue is FreeBSD is still using MySQL 5.7 as the default version. And when the pkg builder is building the packages it will automatically look for the DEFAULT versions and build the packages based on DEFAULT versions.

However there are ways to bypass it but it requires a good time and resources. You have to use your own pkg builder with poudriere where you can build the packages based on the updated DEFAULT versions.

Another way is using the ports tree to build. Although I prefer the previous solution as that is reusable on multiple hosts. So rather than building the ports tree on multiple hosts you can build once and use that pkg repo everywhere else.

And about your reply on top of that. This is a common problem to FreeBSD users who prefer to stay more bleeding edge with ports from mysql*, pgsql*, perl*, ruby*, python*, php*, rust*, mono* and whatever has multiple versions.

I am not sure whether if there is an article or FAQ for this specific problem but these are some head starts https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/ports/#ports-poudriere or https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/testing/#testing-poudriere. I hope this helps.

The version is a bit old which I will update maybe on coming weekend.
